compiled_state.title: As a DSO orchestrator, agent-batch-lifecycle.sh emits 3-tier MAX_AGENTS protocol
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Extend cmd_pre_check() in agent-batch-lifecycle.sh to call check-usage.sh, read orchestration.max_agents config, and emit MAX_AGENTS: unlimited|N|0 (replacing binary 5/1)
**Why**: The sole integration point between usage detection and orchestration skills; all throttle decisions flow through this output
**Scope**:
- IN: check-usage.sh integration, orchestration.max_agents config reading via _read_cfg(), 3-tier output protocol, context-check alignment, sprint-next-batch.sh --limit compatibility
- OUT: Error-reactive fallback (Story 4), CLAUDE.md updates (Story 5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, agent-batch-lifecycle.sh pre-check emits MAX_AGENTS: unlimited when usage is low and orchestration.max_agents is unset; MAX_AGENTS: 1 when check-usage.sh returns exit 1; MAX_AGENTS: 0 when check-usage.sh returns exit 2
  ← Satisfies: "When 5-hour utilization reaches 90%... limit sub-agent dispatch to 1 agent" and "When 5-hour utilization reaches 95%... pause sub-agent dispatch"
- When this story is complete, orchestration.max_agents config key caps the MAX_AGENTS value (e.g., if config says 3 and verdict is unlimited, emit MAX_AGENTS: 3); verified by test with config set and verdict unlimited
  ← Satisfies: "orchestration.max_agents config key caps the upper bound on concurrent sub-agents"
- When this story is complete, context-check exit-11 behavior is aligned with the new 3-tier protocol (exit 11 implies MAX_AGENTS: 1 rather than the old binary model)
  ← Satisfies: "orchestration skills limit sub-agent dispatch to 1 agent at a time"
- When this story is complete, sprint-next-batch.sh --limit parameter accepts unlimited (omit flag or no-cap sentinel) and 0 (returns empty batch) in addition to positive integers
  ← Satisfies: "orchestration skills pause sub-agent dispatch entirely"
- When this story is complete, tests using WORKFLOW_CONFIG injection cover: each of 3 verdicts, config cap interaction, sprint-next-batch.sh --limit edge cases (unlimited, 0, integer), context-check + pre-check interaction
  ← Satisfies: TDD DoD

## Considerations

- [Testing] No existing test harness for agent-batch-lifecycle.sh — need new test infrastructure with WORKFLOW_CONFIG injection and mock check-usage.sh
- [Reliability] CI short-circuit: when check-usage.sh returns no-credentials, pre-check should emit MAX_AGENTS as per config or default behavior
- [Reliability] context-check and pre-check must agree on throttle vocabulary to avoid dual-path conflicts
